label: Saltglass Canopy
description: Saltglass Canopy forms a rare arboreal belt rooted in fissured basalt and crusted with wind-laid halite, creating a forest of dark trunks under pale mineral bloom. Its sparse crown rises in rigid, jointed silhouettes that trap the planet’s thin, mineral-laden haze and filter it into bright bands under Saltharrow’s hard white radiance. The ground layer consists of salt pans broken by stony hummocks and shallow runnels where brine gathers and evaporates, leaving sharp-edged crystalline seams. Exposure to pronounced tidal stresses fractures the basalt into repeating terraces that segment the woodland into distinct, navigable shelves.
